title: Prunus serrulata shujaku
intro: Prunus serrulata shujaku, also known as Japanese flowering cherry or Shujaku cherry, is a deciduous ornamental tree species in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to East Asia. The bark and flowers of P. serrulata shujaku have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with P. serrulata shujaku include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried bark and flower extracts that are used to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
